req.title: Read patient (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can read only patients assigned to them, with 403 returned for unassigned patients and an audit log written on success. Four tests are designed: (1) EP-positive: assigned clinician reads their patient -> 200 + audit; (2) NEG-negative: unassigned clinician reads another patient -> 403; (3) BVA-boundary: clinician reads a patient whose assignment was just removed (edge of assignment boundary) -> 403; (4) STC-boundary: clinician token in an expired/invalid state attempts read -> 401, covering a state transition from valid to expired auth state.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_can_read_patient
preconditions: Valid JWT bearer token is available for 'clinician_a'
actions: Obtain bearer token for clinician_a via <get_auth_token> fixture
Query audit log for the read event
expected: Response body contains resourceType='Patient' and id='patient_001'
Audit log contains an entry for clinician_a reading patient_001
trace: R-HLT-052: clinician reads assigned patient -> resource returned
R-HLT-052: expected outcome -> access audit written
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_assigned_clinician_can_read_patient(api_client, get_auth_token, audit_log_client):
    token = get_auth_token(user="clinician_a", role="clinician")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    patient_id = "patient_001"

    response = api_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert body.get("id") == patient_id

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        actor="clinician_a", resource_id=patient_id, action="read"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit entry not written for successful read"

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_cannot_read_patient
preconditions: Valid JWT bearer token is available for 'clinician_b'
actions: Obtain bearer token for clinician_b via <get_auth_token> fixture
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error indicating access denied
No patient data is returned in the response body
trace: R-HLT-052: error pathway -> 403 when clinician not assigned
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unassigned_clinician_cannot_read_patient(api_client, get_auth_token):
    token = get_auth_token(user="clinician_b", role="clinician")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    patient_id = "patient_002"

    response = api_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "resourceType" not in body or body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", (
        "Patient data must not be exposed on 403"
    )
    if body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome":
        issues = body.get("issue", [])
        assert any(i.get("severity") in ("error", "fatal") for i in issues)

tests.name: test_clinician_denied_after_assignment_removed
preconditions: Clinician 'clinician_c' was previously assigned to patient 'patient_003'
Assignment for clinician_c -> patient_003 has just been revoked
Valid JWT bearer token is available for 'clinician_c'
actions: Revoke assignment of clinician_c from patient_003 via <revoke_assignment> helper
Obtain fresh bearer token for clinician_c via <get_auth_token> fixture
Send GET /Patient/patient_003 with Authorization header immediately after revocation
expected: Access is denied at the exact boundary of assignment removal
No patient resource data is present in the response
trace: R-HLT-052: boundary of clinician assignment -> just-revoked access must be denied
R-HLT-052: error pathway -> 403 when clinician not assigned
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_clinician_denied_after_assignment_removed(
    api_client, get_auth_token, revoke_assignment
):
    patient_id = "patient_003"
    clinician = "clinician_c"

    revoke_assignment(clinician=clinician, patient_id=patient_id)

    token = get_auth_token(user=clinician, role="clinician")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    response = api_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 after assignment revocation, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") != "Patient", "Patient data must not be returned"

tests.name: test_expired_token_cannot_read_patient
preconditions: An expired JWT token for clinician_d is available (token state: expired)
actions: Obtain an expired bearer token for clinician_d via <get_expired_token> helper
Send GET /Patient/patient_004 with the expired Authorization header
expected: Response indicates token is expired or invalid
No patient resource data is present in the response
trace: R-HLT-052: state transition from valid-auth to expired-auth must block access
R-HLT-052: conflicting state transitions must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expired_token_cannot_read_patient(api_client, get_expired_token):
    patient_id = "patient_004"
    expired_token = get_expired_token(user="clinician_d", role="clinician")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {expired_token}"}

    response = api_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for expired token, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") != "Patient", "Patient data must not be returned"
